title: chamaesyce serpillifolia subsp. hirtula
intro: Chamaesyce serpillifolia subsp. hirtula, also known as spurge laurel, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the spurge family (Euphorbiaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce pain, and combat infections. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and acting as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. Chamaesyce serpillifolia subsp. hirtula can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote healing and well-being.
